ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "ssn" TEXT /* Social Security Number of the customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer resides */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Income range of the customer */,
  "family_member_id" TEXT /* ID of the family member in shared accounts */,
  "account_creation_date" TEXT /* Date the customer account was created */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  "communication_preference" TEXT /* Preferred method of communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_communication" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication event */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer involved in the communication */,
  "communication_date" TEXT /* Date of the communication */,
  "communication_type" TEXT /* Type of communication (e.g., email, phone call, in-person) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or details about the communication */,
  "employee_id" TEXT /* ID of the employee who handled the communication */,
  "communication_channel" TEXT /* Channel used for communication */,
  "communication_status" TEXT /* Status of the communication */,
  "follow_up_required" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if follow-up is required */,
  "follow_up_date" TEXT /* Date for the follow-up */,
  "communication_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the communication in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_communication_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_communication_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer who made the purchase */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "product_id" TEXT /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product purchased */,
  "total_amount" TEXT /* Total amount of the purchase */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer */,
  "product_id" TEXT /* ID of the preferred product */,
  "preference_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the preference */,
  "preference_date" TEXT /* Date when the preference was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_preferences_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Detailed permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_satisfaction" (
  "satisfaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satisfaction record */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* ID of the customer */,
  "survey_date" TEXT /* Date when the satisfaction survey was conducted */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("satisfaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_satisfaction_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
column_names: Social Security Number of the customer
Date of birth of the customer
ID of the family member in shared accounts
Date the customer account was created
Unique identifier for each communication event
ID of the customer involved in the communication
Type of communication (e.g., email, phone call, in-person)
Notes or details about the communication
ID of the employee who handled the communication
Flag indicating if follow-up is required
Duration of the communication in minutes
ID of the customer who made the purchase
Date when the preference was recorded
